de.uka.ilkd.key.logic

Class Namespace<E extends Named>

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.Namespace<E>
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.io.Serializable

    public class Namespace<E extends Named>
extends java.lang.Object
implements java.io.Serializable
    A Namespace keeps track of already used Names and the objects carrying these names. These objects have to implement the interface Named. It is possible to have nested namespaces in order to represent different visibility scopes.
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      Namespace()
      Construct an empty Namespace without a parent namespace.
      Namespace(Namespace<E> parent)
      Construct a Namespace that uses parent as a fallback for finding symbols not defined in this one.

    • Constructor Detail

      • Namespace

        public Namespace()
        Construct an empty Namespace without a parent namespace.

      • Namespace

        public Namespace(Namespace<E> parent)
        Construct a Namespace that uses parent as a fallback for finding symbols not defined in this one.

    • Method Detail

      • add

        public void add(E sym)
        Adds the object sym to this Namespace. If an object with the same name is already there, it is quietly replaced by sym. Use addSafely() instead if possible. TODO:The problem of saving to localSym, symbols, and symbolRefs is not solved yet. (This is no longer self-explanatory. mu 2016) If the local table is empty, then the new symbol is added as "singleton map". This has been adapted from an earlier implementation, done for memory efficiency reasons: Many namespaces only contain a single element; no need to allocate a hash map. The hash map is only created when the 2nd element is added. This is not threadsafe.

      • add

        public void add(Namespace<E> source)

      • add

        public void add(java.lang.Iterable<? extends E> list)

      • addSafely

        public void addSafely(E sym)
        Adds the object sym to this namespace. Throws a runtime exception if an object with the same name is already there.

      • addSafely

        public void addSafely(java.lang.Iterable<? extends E> names)

      • remove

        public void remove(Name name)
        Remove a name from the namespace. Removal is not delegated to the parent namespace.
        Parameters:
        name - non-null name whose symbol is to be removed.

      • lookupLocally

        protected E lookupLocally(Name name)

      • extended

        public Namespace<E> extended(E sym)
        creates a new Namespace that has this as parent, and contains an entry for sym.
        Returns:
        the new Namespace

      • extended

        public Namespace<E> extended(java.lang.Iterable<? extends E> ext)

      • lookup

        @Nullable
public E lookup(Name name)
        looks if a registered object is declared in this namespace, if negative it asks its parent
        Parameters:
        name - a Name representing the name of the symbol to look for
        Returns:
        Object with name "name" or null if no such an object has been found

      • lookup

        public E lookup(java.lang.String name)
        Convenience method to look up.

      • elements

        public java.util.Collection<E> elements()
        returns list of the elements (not the keys) in this namespace (not about the one of the parent)
        Returns:
        the list of the named objects

      • allElements

        public java.util.Collection<E> allElements()

      • parent

        public Namespace<E> parent()
        returns the fall-back Namespace of this Namespace, i.e. the one where symbols are looked up that are not found in this one.

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object

      • set
